answer: The default setting for S3 Object Ownership is bucket owner enforced, and it enforces that all ACLs are disabled and the bucket owner owns all the objects in the bucket and manages access to data exclusively using access management policies.
PUT requests that contain other ACLs (for example, custom grants to certain AWS accounts) fail and return an HTTP status code 400 (Bad Request) with the error code AccessControlListNotSupported.
Server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed keys (SSE-S3)
